issueComments.body:
[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/pull/163?utm_source=github_walkthrough&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=change_stack) > [!WARNING] > ## Review limit reached > > You’ve reached a temporary PR review limit under our [Fair Usage Limits Policy](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#fair-usage-limits-policy).
> Your recent review volume is higher than typical usage, so adaptive limits are currently applied. > > **Next review available in:** **21 minutes** > > Enable **[usage-based reviews](https://app.coderabbit.ai/settings/billing?tab=usage&orgId=884645f9-892f-4c20-9a7a-21ddb620dd83)** in Billing to review now. Otherwise, wait until the next included review is available. > You're only billed for reviews past your plan's rate limits ($0.25/file). > >
> How can I continue? > > After more reviews become available, a review can be triggered using the `@coderabbitai review` command as a PR comment. Alternatively, push new commits to this PR. > > To avoid repeated limits, reduce automatic review volume by pausing incremental auto-reviews earlier, using label-based review opt-in, excluding WIP or generated PR titles, or requesting reviews manually when the PR is ready. If your team needs uninterrupted high-volume reviews, an organization admin can enable usage-based reviews. > >
> > >
> How do review limits work? > > CodeRabbit enforces per-developer PR review limits for each organization. Most developers receive the normal plan review availability. > > For paid Pro and Pro+ PR reviews, CodeRabbit uses adaptive limits for sustained high-volume activity. When a developer's recent PR review activity reaches the 95th percentile or higher among CodeRabbit users, additional reviews become available more gradually as earlier reviews age out of the rolling window. > > Please refer [docs](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/management/plans#rate-limits) for additional details. > >
> >
> Review details > >
> ⚙️ Run configuration > > **Configuration used**: Organization UI > > **Review profile**: CHILL > > **Plan**: Pro > > **Run ID**: `bdaa643d-db80-4849-b0e1-b164e793dd43` > >
> >
> 📥 Commits > > Reviewing files that changed from the base of the PR and between 7545616a5e8adeb7e73edd0a8e7f8ecac0d4d65f and 50e1908b2dd6f7e7b171c544450a3faa8ad035a5. > >
> >
> 📒 Files selected for processing (3) > > * `plugins/pr/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` > * `plugins/pr/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json` > * `plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md` > >
> >
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough The PR adds a Claude and Cursor `pr` plugin, registers its manifests, and defines a `/pr` skill that validates the current branch, creates a pull request with generated content, then runs up to three CI and review babysitting passes. ### Changes **Pull request workflow** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Plugin registration and manifests**
`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, `.cursor-plugin/marketplace.json`, `plugins/pr/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, `plugins/pr/.cursor-plugin/plugin.json`|Registers the `pr` plugin and adds matching version `0.1.0` manifests with author and workflow metadata.| |**Skill contract and preflight**
`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md`|Defines `/pr` arguments and dependencies, reads composed skills, validates repository and branch state, prevents duplicate PRs, and pushes the branch when needed.| |**PR creation and babysitting**
`plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md`|Generates the PR title and body, creates the PR with `gh pr create`, captures its URL, and runs up to three `cb-babysit` passes with defined stop conditions.| **Estimated code review effort:** 3 (Moderate) | ~20 minutes ### Sequence Diagram(s) ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant GitRepository participant pr-summary-writer participant gh participant cb-babysit GitRepository->>GitRepository: Validate branch, upstream, working tree, and existing PR pr-summary-writer->>gh: Supply generated title and body gh->>GitRepository: Create pull request GitRepository-->>gh: Return pull request URL cb-babysit->>gh: Run CI and review feedback pass gh-->>cb-babysit: Return pass outcome ```
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 5
✅ Passed checks (5 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title clearly summarizes the main change: adding the /pr skill that creates a PR and babysits it three times. | | Docstring Coverage | ✅ Passed | No functions found in the changed files to evaluate docstring coverage. Skipping docstring coverage check. | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✨ Finishing Touches
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `emdash/create-a-pr-skill-imh6y`
--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands.

issueComments.author: paulbaranowski
issueComments.body: ## Review-body findings ### Agree - Detect the repository’s actual default branch (not only `main`/`master`) — fixed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/3d5f3de773385cf1f58f30cc94edd9928e8230f0. - Compare ahead/behind before push — fixed in the same commit. - Shell-safe title / args / `--body-file -` for `gh pr create` — fixed in the same commit. ``` 3b31284278db01d5 ``` 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

issueComments.author: paulbaranowski
issueComments.body: ## Review-body findings ### Agree - Skip-create path undefined when an existing PR is chosen — fixed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/be5654ed10aaa7ff4411d2b464a83e27312d07f6. - Apostrophe breaks `TITLE='…'` assignment — fixed in the same commit (heredoc → variable). ``` 3bbb42f2446c20c0 ``` 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

issueComments.author: paulbaranowski
issueComments.body: ## Review-body findings ### Agree - Rules omit ahead-of-upstream push — fixed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/2a9f25f28408686e4e021204d20415d8a9befc22 so the Do list matches Phase 1. ``` a13e8101265f139f ``` 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

issueComments.author: paulbaranowski
issueComments.body: ## Review-body findings ### Agree - Babysit-only path skips push — fixed in https://github.com/paulbaranowski/wild-horses/commit/50e1908b2dd6f7e7b171c544450a3faa8ad035a5 so option (a) pushes when ahead/no upstream before Phase 4. ``` 540e02ddf6edabe3 ``` 🤖 cb-babysit:addressed v1 core@3.16.1

reviewBodyComments.author: coderabbitai
reviewBodyComments.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 3**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@plugins/pr/skills/pr/SKILL.md`:
- Around line 49-52: Update the stop conditions in the PR workflow documentation
to detect the repository’s actual default branch via `gh repo view` or the
remote HEAD, rather than checking only `main` and `master`; prevent PR creation
when currently on that branch without a feature branch, while preserving the
existing dirty-working-tree checks.
- Around line 55-59: Update the branching guidance around the upstream check to
compare local HEAD with its upstream ref, not merely confirm that an upstream
exists. Use an ahead/behind comparison such as git status --short --branch or
git rev-list to detect local commits, and run git push -u origin HEAD whenever
the branch is ahead or lacks an upstream before creating the PR.
- Around line 73-84: Update the PR creation command to pass the generated title
through a validated, shell-safe variable and invoke it as a quoted argument;
parse validated arguments into an array to prevent word splitting and glob
expansion, converting bare branch names to a base-branch flag. Replace
command-substituted body handling with a single-quoted heredoc piped via
`--body-file -`, while preserving PR URL capture after `gh pr create` in the
documented workflow.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR:

- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "4b0d0e0a-96d7-4f10-b296-3a18ea78f0b9"} --> Push a commit to this branch (recommended)
- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "ff5b1114-7d8c-49e6-8ac1-43f82af23a33"} --> Create a new PR with the fixes

</details>
